last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

lines: Context : hr: {k : key, n : nonce, r : poly_in, s : poly_out, b : block,
post = poly1305 r s (topol a c) = genpoly1305 (cc gs0) k0 n0 (topol a0 c0)

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.tactic_shape: Inspect proof obligation stack: direct_procedure_call / OCC / statement 1.
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

moves.tactic_shape: Inspect one-sided call-site map: OCC on side 1 at statement 1.
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

moves.title: Residual closing tactic
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: call sites are visible at the frontier; residual statements remain after a call frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.right_first: no matching right-side call at this frontier
first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: The visible frontier is one-sided; use a one-sided tactic, alignment step, or seq cut before applying a symmetric tactic.

rows.right: no matching right-side call at this frontier
rows.proof_read: This is a call frontier; compare the procedures/oracle wrappers before choosing a named call or invariant call.

rows.right: no right-side residual statement after this call frontier
rows.proof_read: The left side has residual statements after the live call frontier; their effects are not part of the callee obligation.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

checks: Keep one-sided instrumentation as a map, not as a tactic recipe.

named_handles.why_visible: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
limitations: The tactic shape is hidden until the handle is callable at the current frontier.

frontier_blockers.right: no right-side residual statement after this call frontier
frontier_blockers.effect: Residual statements remain after the live call frontier; their effects are not part of the callee obligation.

rows.right: no right-side residual statement after this call frontier
rows.proof_read: The left side has residual statements after the live call frontier; their effects are not part of the callee obligation.

residual_frontier.effect: These statements remain after the live call frontier and are checked after the call returns.
limitations: The surface reports suffix structure only; it does not choose whether to use a call, inline, wp, or another local tactic.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: No fresh recommendation is available; parse the current goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: The current procedure frontier exposes a call route.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, while, seq, rnd, or rewrite.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid one-sided hoare/phoare loop form.
Mid-proof suffix may need indexed `wp` to absorb a tail before a cut.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp` or `wp`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken the pre/postcondition before closing.
A sampling step may need `rnd` to reduce it to a distribution/probability fact.
